questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to replace the underlined segment. He thinks his new plan will undoubtedly produce the desired result.
questions.explanation: D)Bear fruit(idiom) – Produce the desired result फल देना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence During his short period when he stays in Paris, he immersed himself in the city's art and culture.
questions.explanation: B)Sojourn(noun) – a temporary stay, especially when traveling. अस्थायी प्रवास

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Due to _________ 'eve teasing', parents conclude that it is not worth the danger of sending their daughters to school.
questions.explanation: D)Widespread'का use होगा क्योंकि "widespread" का अर्थ होता है व्यापक रूप से फैला हुआ। sentence में यह बताया गया है कि 'eve teasing' की व्यापकता के कारण माता-पिता अपनी बेटियों को स्कूल भेजने के खतरे को नहीं उठाना चाहते। इसलिए 'widespread' यहाँ सही optionहै। जबकि: 'Rectified' का अर्थ है किसी समस्या को ठीक करना, जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता। 'Localised' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को सीमित क्षेत्र में केंद्रित करना, लेकिन यहां समस्या की व्यापकता पर जोर है। 'Narrow' का अर्थ है संकीर्ण या सीमित, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। Widespread'will be used because it means "extensively prevalent." The sentence implies that due to the extensive prevalence of 'eve teasing,' parents find it risky to send their daughters to school, making 'widespread' the correct option. Whereas: 'Rectified' means to correct a problem, which does not fit the context. 'Localised' means confined to a specific area, but the sentence emphasizes the prevalence, not restriction. 'Narrow' means limited or restricted, which is not appropriate in this context.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. She was overly concerned about the results, which affected her ability to focus.
questions.explanation: C) Overly(adverb) – Excessively, Unduly, Extremely, Unnecessarily, Abnormally अत्यधिक Antonym:Moderately(adverb) – Reasonably, Slightly, Fairly, Adequately संयमित Loud(adjective) – Producing a lot of noise, high in volume. ऊँचा Distinct(adjective) – Clearly different, noticeable, well-defined. स्पष्ट Cacophonous(adjective) – Harsh-sounding, unpleasant, discordant. कर्कश

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A) The monthly average price of the Indian basket of crude began to increase after Russia invaded Ukraine in February 2022. B) It peaked at $116/bbl in June 2022. C) By May 2023, the price crashed to $75. D) But India’s retail fuel prices have remained frozen – no changes since May 22, 2022.
questions.explanation: B) ABCD A:"The monthly average price of the Indian basket of crude began to increase after Russia invaded Ukraine in February 2022." This sentence serves as a good starting point, providing the background and context for the subsequent events B:"It peaked at $116/bbl in June 2022." This follows logically from sentence A, indicating the progression of crude oil prices over time C:"By May 2023, the price crashed to $75." This continues the timeline from sentence B, showcasing a drastic change in the price, adding further detail to the sequence of events D:"But India’s retail fuel prices have remained frozen – no changes since May 22, 2022." This sentence introduces a contrast with the prior sentences and wraps up the narrative by pointing out the discrepancy between international crude oil prices and India's retail fuel prices

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: B) The correct spelling isEntertainment, which means "the action of providing or being provided with amusement or enjoyment" (मनोरंजन)

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The controversial topic seemed to polarise the audience into two opposing groups.
questions.explanation: B) Polarise(verb) – Divide, Split, Separate, Fragment, Alienate ध्रुवीकरण करना Synonym: Divide(verb) – Split, separate, polarise, fragment, disunite विभाजित करना Unite(verb) – Join, combine, unify, connect, merge एकजुट करना Harmonise(verb) – Reconcile, coordinate, blend, integrate, synchronise सामंजस्य बिठाना Reconcile(verb) – Harmonise, settle, resolve, mend, pacify सुलह करना

questions.question: Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. BBC Media has shown exponenteal growth in their revenue this year.
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of'exponenteal'is'exponential', which means “increasing rapidly by successive increments or multiples” (अत्यधिक तीव्रता से बढ़ना).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The detail given by the instructor was the ______ one.
questions.explanation: C)'Most updated'का use सही है क्योंकि "most updated" का अर्थ है सबसे नया या हालिया जानकारी। यहाँ sentence में एक विशेष जानकारी की बात हो रही है जो सबसे नई और प्रासंगिक है। 'Most updated' adjective के superlative degree को दर्शाता है, जो सही है क्योंकि यह "the detail" (singular noun) को modify करता है। "Updating" एक present participle है, जिसका अर्थ है 'update करने की प्रक्रिया', यह sentence में adjective की तरह use नहीं हो सकता। "Updates" plural noun है, लेकिन sentence में एक singular noun ("the detail") की बात हो रही है, इसलिए यह contextually गलत है। 'Most updated' is correct because it is a superlative adjective that means the most recent or latest. The sentence refers to a specific detail provided by the instructor, which is described as the most current or up-to-date information. Superlative forms like "most updated" are used when comparing one thing against all others, which fits here since "the detail" is singular and specific. updating – "Updating" is a present participle, which means the process of updating. It cannot act as an adjective to describe "the detail" in this context. "Updates" is a plural noun, but the sentence refers to a singular "detail," making it grammatically incorrect.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The architect presented the contour of the building, highlighting its unique design and structure.
questions.explanation: C) Contour(noun) – Outline, Framework, Structure, Shape, Plan रूपरेखा Synonym: Outline(noun) – Contour, framework, structure, shape, sketch रूपरेखा Interior(noun) – Inside, core, inner part, innards, contents आंतरिक भाग Color(noun) – Hue, shade, tint, tone, pigment रंग Texture(noun) – Surface, feel, grain, weave, consistency बनावट

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Tom is a person who searches for and collects discarded items, turning what others throw away into valuable treasures for his community.
questions.explanation: A)Scavenger(noun) – a person who searches for and collects discarded items. कचरा बीनने वाला व्यक्ति

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Due to its lethal potential, handling the chemical requires extreme caution.
questions.explanation: A) Lethal(adjective) – Deadly, fatal, dangerous, devastating, killer घातक Antonym: Harmless(adjective) – Not able or likely to cause harm. हानिरहित Deadly(adjective) – Causing or able to cause death. घातक Dangerous(adjective) – Able or likely to cause harm or injury. खतरनाक Devastating(adjective) – Highly destructive or damaging. विनाशकारी

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The looming deadline for the project has everyone working overtime to meet expectations.
questions.explanation: D) Looming(adjective) – Impending, Approaching, Forthcoming, Imminent, Upcoming मंडराता Synonym: Impending(adjective) – Looming, Approaching, Forthcoming, Imminent, Upcoming (आसन्न) Distant(adjective) – Faraway, Remote, Far-off, Far, Removed (दूर) Remote(adjective) – Distant, Faraway, Isolated, Far-off, Unlikely (दूरस्थ) Unlikely(adjective) – Improbable, Doubtful, Unbelievable, Implausible, Questionable (असंभावित)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Out of spirits
questions.explanation: D)Out of spirits(idiom) – Gloomy (उदास)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. The White man had to swallow his bombast and have completed the journey in mortified.
questions.explanation: C) 'have completed' के बदले 'complete' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'had to' एक Modal Expression है, जिसके बाद हमेशा Verb का Base Form (Bare Infinitive) आता है ।जैसे— Incorrect: He had to have finished the work. Correct: He had to finish the work. 'in mortified' के बदले 'in mortification' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ एक Noun की आवश्यकता है, जो 'journey' को और स्पष्ट करे। 'Mortified' एक Adjective है, जो गलत है। 'complete the journey in mortification'will replace'have completed the journey in mortified'because 'had to' requires the base form of the verb ('complete'), and 'mortification' (a Noun) is needed instead of 'mortified' (an Adjective).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined phrase. In the absence of government rule, riots have broken out all over the country and everything is in a state of complete chaos.
questions.explanation: D)Anarchy(noun) – A state of society where there is no government or authority, leading to complete chaos or lawlessness. अराजकता Dictatorship(noun) – A form of government in which a single leader or party has absolute power. तानाशाही Monarchy(noun) – A system of government ruled by a king or queen. राजशाही Oligarchy(noun) – A system of government where a small group of people hold power. कुलीनतंत्र

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Like a dying duck in a thunderstorm
questions.explanation: A)Like a dying duck in a thunderstorm– Dejected or hopeless अत्यधिक निराश, उदास या असहाय दिखना (व्यक्ति की पूरी निराशा और दयनीय स्थिति को दर्शाता है, जैसे एक मरता हुआ बत्तख गरज और बारिश में होता है।)

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Japan’s prime minister, Shinzo Abe, urged G20 leaders to / work together to defuse trade / and political tensions, describing it as / threats to global economic growth and stability.
options: Japan’s prime minister, Shinzo Abe, urged G20 leaders to
and political tensions, describing it as
threats to global economic growth and stability.
questions.explanation: C) 'describing it as' के बदले 'describing them as' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ 'it' का प्रयोग 'trade and political tensions' (plural) के लिए हो रहा है, जो गलत है। Plural noun के लिए 'them' का प्रयोग करेंगे; जैसे— The teacher gave the students their books and asked them to read. • 'describing them as' will be used instead of 'describing it as' because here 'it' is being used for 'trade and political tensions' (plural), which is incorrect. For plural noun, we will use 'them'; Like— The teacher gave the students their books and asked them to read.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Giving the large base of tanks and (A) / reservoirs in Greater Chennai, (B) / prudent rainfall management can help it through (C) / withering summers and weak monsoons. (D)
options: Giving the large base of tanks and
prudent rainfall management can help it through
questions.explanation: A)'Giving'के बदले'Given'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँpreposition/participle errorहै।"Given"(दिए हुए/को देखते हुए) का प्रयोग किसी स्थिति को स्वीकार करते हुए किया जाता है, जबकि"Giving"(देते हुए) यहाँ अर्थपूर्ण नहीं है; जैसे—Given the heavy traffic, we should leave early. •'Given'will be used instead of'Giving'because there is apreposition/participle error."Given"means"considering/taking into account", whereas"Giving"(to give) does not fit here; Like—Given the heavy traffic, we should leave early.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A) So, as our MPs get ready to work from a swanky new office, they and, even more, their party leaders must ask what use is India’s new House for India if it can’t house thoughtful, civil debates and pass well-scrutinised laws that make things better for citizens. B) If the splendour and hi-tech amenities reflected new ambitions, the war of tweets and statements pointed to a bitter political divide that can act as a drag on that ambition. C) A grand ceremony and sharp political rhetoric greeted the new Parliament. D) Which is to say it would be tragic were the ways of the old Parliament carry over to the new one.
questions.explanation: A) CBAD C:"A grand ceremony and sharp political rhetoric greeted the new Parliament." This sentence seems like a natural introduction to the topic. It is setting the stage for the discussion by mentioning the new Parliament and the grand ceremonyB:This sentence elaborates on the scenario introduced in sentence C, discussing the new ambitions, the splendour and the hi-tech amenities of the new Parliament, as well as the war of tweets and statements, and the bitter political divide. It provides more context to the situation and brings in a conflict. A:This sentence is connected to the preceding one (B) as it continues the line of thought about the implications of the new Parliament and the duties of the MPs and party leaders. The use of the word "So" implies a conclusion or a solution to a previously stated problem, which in this case is the bitter political divide mentioned in B D:Finally, this sentence concludes the passage with a cautionary statement. "Which is to say" implies a summarization or restatement of the argument made in previous sentences, hence, this sentence seems most fitting at the end.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. George Patton Jr said, “I don’t measure man’s success by how high he climbs, but how high he bounces back _____1_____ he hits the bottom.” Emotional and physical _____2______ often _____3______ us. Stages of life are soaked with such experiences. As a child, we were frustrated ____4______not getting things that we wanted dearly. In youth we may have been bullied and rejected. And so the story goes on in our working and home lives, as we grow older. _______5_____, each time, we manage to recover, after a few gaps of silent suffering. During these gaps we intentionally or unintentionally, disconnect and withdraw ourselves from outward happenings, silently submitting and somehow connecting with something deeper and mysterious that overtakes us, and finally gathering the strength to bounce back. This mysterious power is what drove us to rise above all challenges and carry on with life to the best of our abilities. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: A) 'When' का use होगा क्योंकि "when" का अर्थ होता है किसी समय या घटना का वर्णन करना, जो यहाँ सही है। Sentence में कहा गया है कि एक व्यक्ति निचले स्तर पर पहुंचने के बाद कैसे उठता है, इसलिए 'when' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Which' और 'Whose' का प्रयोग वस्तु या व्यक्ति की पहचान के लिए होता है और 'Where' का अर्थ है किसी स्थान का वर्णन करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'When' will be used because it refers to the description of a time or event, which fits here. The sentence describes how a person rebounds after reaching the bottom, making 'when' appropriate. Whereas, 'Which' and 'Whose' are used for identifying objects or persons, and 'Where' refers to a location, which do not fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. George Patton Jr said, “I don’t measure man’s success by how high he climbs, but how high he bounces back _____1_____ he hits the bottom.” Emotional and physical _____2______ often _____3______ us. Stages of life are soaked with such experiences. As a child, we were frustrated ____4______not getting things that we wanted dearly. In youth we may have been bullied and rejected. And so the story goes on in our working and home lives, as we grow older. _______5_____, each time, we manage to recover, after a few gaps of silent suffering. During these gaps we intentionally or unintentionally, disconnect and withdraw ourselves from outward happenings, silently submitting and somehow connecting with something deeper and mysterious that overtakes us, and finally gathering the strength to bounce back. This mysterious power is what drove us to rise above all challenges and carry on with life to the best of our abilities. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: D) 'Setbacks' का use होगा क्योंकि "setbacks" का अर्थ होता है बाधाएँ या प्रतिकूल परिस्थितियाँ, जो यहाँ सही है। Passage में mention है कि जीवन के विभिन्न चरण ऐसी चुनौतियों से भरे होते हैं, इसलिए 'setbacks' यहाँ सही है। 'Knocks' का अर्थ होता है आघात या मार, 'Enhancements' का अर्थ होता है सुधार, और 'Improves' का अर्थ है बेहतर बनाना, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Setbacks' will be used because it means obstacles or adverse conditions, which fits here. The passage describes how various stages of life are filled with such challenges, making 'setbacks' appropriate. 'Knocks' implies impacts or hits, 'Enhancements' means improvements, and 'Improves' means to make better, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. George Patton Jr said, “I don’t measure man’s success by how high he climbs, but how high he bounces back _____1_____ he hits the bottom.” Emotional and physical _____2______ often _____3______ us. Stages of life are soaked with such experiences. As a child, we were frustrated ____4______not getting things that we wanted dearly. In youth we may have been bullied and rejected. And so the story goes on in our working and home lives, as we grow older. _______5_____, each time, we manage to recover, after a few gaps of silent suffering. During these gaps we intentionally or unintentionally, disconnect and withdraw ourselves from outward happenings, silently submitting and somehow connecting with something deeper and mysterious that overtakes us, and finally gathering the strength to bounce back. This mysterious power is what drove us to rise above all challenges and carry on with life to the best of our abilities. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: A) 'Overwhelm' का use होगा क्योंकि "overwhelm" का अर्थ होता है बहुत अधिक प्रभावित करना या किसी चीज़ से दब जाना, जो यहाँ फिट बैठता है। passage में mention है कि बाधाएँ (setbacks) हमें प्रभावित करती हैं, इसलिए 'overwhelm' यहाँ सही है। 'Succumb' और 'Yield' का अर्थ होता है हार मान लेना या समर्पण कर देना, और 'Sympathize' का अर्थ है सहानुभूति दिखाना, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Overwhelm' will be used because it means to be heavily affected or overpowered by something, which fits here. The passage describes how setbacks affect us, making 'overwhelm' appropriate. 'Succumb' and 'Yield' imply giving in or surrendering, and 'Sympathize' means to show empathy, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. George Patton Jr said, “I don’t measure man’s success by how high he climbs, but how high he bounces back _____1_____ he hits the bottom.” Emotional and physical _____2______ often _____3______ us. Stages of life are soaked with such experiences. As a child, we were frustrated ____4______not getting things that we wanted dearly. In youth we may have been bullied and rejected. And so the story goes on in our working and home lives, as we grow older. _______5_____, each time, we manage to recover, after a few gaps of silent suffering. During these gaps we intentionally or unintentionally, disconnect and withdraw ourselves from outward happenings, silently submitting and somehow connecting with something deeper and mysterious that overtakes us, and finally gathering the strength to bounce back. This mysterious power is what drove us to rise above all challenges and carry on with life to the best of our abilities. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: C) 'By' का use होगा क्योंकि "by" का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ के कारण होना, जो यहाँ फिट बैठता है। Passage में mention है कि बचपन में हमें वह चीज़ें न मिलने पर निराशा होती थी जिन्हें हम बहुत चाहते थे, इसलिए 'by' यहाँ सही है। 'By' will be used because it implies being affected due to something, which fits here. The passage states that as children, we were frustrated by not getting things we dearly wanted, making 'by' the appropriate choice. 'With', 'On', and 'To' do not fit grammatically in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. George Patton Jr said, “I don’t measure man’s success by how high he climbs, but how high he bounces back _____1_____ he hits the bottom.” Emotional and physical _____2______ often _____3______ us. Stages of life are soaked with such experiences. As a child, we were frustrated ____4______not getting things that we wanted dearly. In youth we may have been bullied and rejected. And so the story goes on in our working and home lives, as we grow older. _______5_____, each time, we manage to recover, after a few gaps of silent suffering. During these gaps we intentionally or unintentionally, disconnect and withdraw ourselves from outward happenings, silently submitting and somehow connecting with something deeper and mysterious that overtakes us, and finally gathering the strength to bounce back. This mysterious power is what drove us to rise above all challenges and carry on with life to the best of our abilities. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: D) 'Yet' का use होगा क्योंकि "yet" का अर्थ होता है फिर भी या तथापि, जो यहाँ सही है। Passage में कहा गया है कि भले ही हम दुःखी हों और कुछ समय के लिए खुद को अलग कर लें, हम फिर भी उठ खड़े होते हैं, इसलिए 'yet' यहाँ सही है। 'Still' का अर्थ होता है अभी भी, 'Similarly' का अर्थ होता है उसी तरह से, और 'Besides' का अर्थ होता है इसके अलावा, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'Yet' will be used because it means nonetheless or however, which fits here. The passage indicates that even though we suffer and withdraw for a time, we manage to recover, making 'yet' the appropriate choice. 'Still' means still, 'Similarly' implies in the same manner, and 'Besides' means in addition to, which don't fit in this context.
